title: -Contract: Koios Engineering, Inc. for Adeline Street, Woolsey Street, et al. Sanitary Sewer Rehabilitation Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution approving plans and specifications for the Sanitary Sewer Project
located on Adeline Street, Woolsey Street, et al., and:
 Accept the bid from the lowest, responsive and responsible bidder, Koios
Engineering, Inc., and
 Authorize the City Manager to execute a contract and any amendments,
extensions, or other change orders until completion of the project in accordance
with the approved plans and specifications, in an amount not to exceed
$3,931,888.95, which includes a 10% contingency of $357,444.45.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding is available in the FY 2026 Sanitary Sewer Fund (611).
Low bid by Koios Engineering.................................$3,574,444.50
10% Contingency...........................................................$357,444.45
Total construction cost $3,931,888.95
sections.current_situation:
An Invitation for Bids (Specification. No. 26-11758-C) was released on December 12,
2025, and bids were opened on January 6, 2026. Nine non-local bids were received
ranging from $3,574,444.50 to $5,979,156.50, as detailed in Attachment 3 (Bid Results).
The City Staff Estimate for the project was $5.31 million. Koios Engineering, Inc. (Koios
Engineering) of San Francisco, CA was the lowest responsive and responsible bidder
with a bid of $3,574,444.50. References for Koios Engineering proved satisfactory
and staff recommend the contract be awarded to Koios Engineering
This sanitary sewer project supports the City's Strategic Plan goals of providing state-of-
the-art, well-maintained infrastructure amenities and the goal of protecting the
environment.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

Contract: Koios Engineering, Inc. for Sanitary Sewer Rehabilitation CONSENT CALENDAR
Adeline Street, Woolsey Street, et al. February 24, 2026
sections.background:
To remain compliant with the Consent Decree (CD) issued on September 22, 2014, by
the State Regional Water Quality Control Board, the City has implemented a long-term
mandated Sanitary Sewer Capital Improvement Program to eliminate sanitary sewer
overflows (SSOs) and to reduce stormwater infiltration and inflow into the sanitary sewer
system. Under this program, the City utilizes a comprehensive asset management
approach based on complex and evolving hydrologic and hydraulic modeling and
condition assessments to repair, replace, or upgrade the City's portion of the sanitary
sewer system. These actions will assist the City and East Bay Municipal Utility District
(EBMUD) in eliminating discharges from EBMUD’s wet weather facilities by the end of
2035.
This is the twelfth year of the twenty-two-year CD program, which stipulates that the City
shall perform collection system repair and rehabilitation to control infiltration and inflow.
This supports ongoing work previously identified in the City's Sanitary Sewer
Management Plan (SSMP) and Asset Management Implementation Plan (AMIP).
The project will rehabilitate sanitary sewer collection systems in various areas of the City,
as shown on the Location Map (Attachment 2). These sanitary sewer collection systems
need immediate rehabilitation to prevent pipe failures, sewer blockages, and leakage
problems.
Planned work includes the rehabilitation of approximately 7,131 linear feet of sanitary
sewer mains varying in size from 6-inch to 21-inch diameter; maintenance hole
rehabilitation; replacement of 4-inch and 6-inch diameter sewer laterals; and other related
work. To reduce traffic impacts, minimize inconvenience to the public, and reduce costs,
a majority of the work will be performed using trenchless methods. These trenchless
methods allow replacement of pipelines without the need for a traditional open trench
construction. These methods allow for cost savings and avoid street closures and traffic
disruptions caused by ope
sections.rationale:
Contracted services are required for the specialized services required for this project, as
the City lacks the in-house resources needed to complete scheduled sanitary sewer
rehabilitation and replacement projects. Further, the City must take timely action to
address urgent/emergent sewer repairs without delay. Finally, subject to fines and
stipulated penalties, the Consent Decree requires the City to complete sanitary sewer
mains rehabilitation and replacement at an average annual rate of no less than 22,120
feet on a three-fiscal-year rolling average. This project is essential for the City to maintain
compliance with the three-year rolling average.
sections.alternatives:
Taking no action will result in the City violating the CD and becoming subject to fines and
penalties. No reasonable alternative exists as the City's sanitary sewer pipelines are in
poor condition and need timely rehabilitation to prevent an increased probability of
infiltration and inflows, leakages, and sanitary sewer overflows.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, Public Works, (510) 981-6339
Ricardo Salcedo, Supervising Civil Engineer, Public Works, (510) 981-6407
Adadu Yemane, Associate Civil Engineer, Public Works, (510) 981-6413

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: KOIOS ENGINEERING, INC. FOR SANITARY SEWER
REHABILITATION – ADELINE STREET, WOOLSEY STREET, ET AL.
WHEREAS, the Sanitary Sewer Project is part of the City's on-going Sanitary Sewer
Capital Improvement Program to rehabilitate or replace the aging and deteriorated
sanitary sewer system; and
WHEREAS, the Capital Improvement Program is a requirement of compliance with the
National Pollution Discharge Elimination System Permit (NPDES) and California Regional
Water Quality Control Board Consent Decree; and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the staff nor the equipment necessary to undertake this
Sanitary Sewer Rehabilitation Project and other urgent/emergent sewer repairs; and
WHEREAS, on December 12, 2025, the City released an Invitation for Bids
(Specification No. 26-11758-C) for sanitary sewer rehabilitation and replacement; and
WHEREAS, on January 6, 2026, the City received nine bids, and Koios Engineering, Inc.
was found to be the lowest responsive and responsible bidder; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the FY 2026 budget in the Sanitary Sewer Fund (611);
and
WHEREAS, the City filed a Notice of Exemption on November 13, 2025, declaring this
work is exempt from the California Environmental Quality Act.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Plans and Specifications No. 26-11758-C for Sanitary Sewer Rehabilitation – Adeline
Street, Woolsey Street, et al. are approved.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley authorizes the City
Manager to execute a contract and any amendments with Koios Engineering, Inc., until
completion of the project in accordance with the approved plans and specifications for the
Sanitary Sewer Rehabilitation Project located on Adeline Street, Woolsey Street et al. in
an amount not to exceed $3,931,888.95 which includes a 10% contingency for
unforeseen circumstances. A record signature co
